chapter: Chapter 2: The Social War and the Italian Question (91-88 BCE)

questions.question: How did the distribution of war spoils reflect the unequal relationship between Rome and its allies?
options: Allies received spoils first as compensation for danger
Spoils were divided equally regardless of status
Roman citizens typically received larger shares than allies
Allies kept all spoils from battles they fought alone
The Senate alone decided and kept most spoils

questions.question: Why did ordinary Roman citizens oppose extending citizenship to the Italian allies?
options: They feared the allies would vote to abolish the Republic
They saw citizenship extension as diluting their status
They believed Italians were culturally inferior
They wanted to maintain military superiority over allies
They objected to sharing religious practices

questions.question: What made the Social War different from earlier Italian rebellions against Rome?
options: It was the first rebellion to receive foreign support
It lasted longer than any previous Italian conflict
The rebels sought to destroy Rome rather than gain rights
Only the poorest communities participated in the revolt
The rebels were prosperous and militarily trained in Roman tactics

questions.question: What strategic purpose did Rome's citizenship grants serve during the Social War?
options: They were meant to recruit allied soldiers into Roman legions
They aimed to divide the rebel coalition by satisfying moderates
They were designed to punish communities that stayed loyal
They sought to gain support from Greek cities in the south
They were intended to end the war before winter arrived

questions.question: How did the Senate attempt to limit the political impact of the new Italian citizens?
options: By requiring them to serve ten years in the army first
By excluding them from holding magistracies for a generation
By making their citizenship conditional on property ownership
By registering them in only eight of thirty-five voting tribes
By creating separate assemblies for Italian citizens

questions.question: Which consul was killed fighting the Marsi in 90 BCE?
options: Lucius Julius Caesar
Gnaeus Pompeius Strabo
Publius Rutilius Lupus
Lucius Cornelius Sulla
Quintus Servilius Caepio

questions.question: What role did Gaius Marius play in the Social War?
options: He commanded the main Roman army throughout the war
He negotiated the citizenship grants with rebel leaders
He led the final assault that ended the rebellion
He came out of retirement and salvaged a difficult situation
He remained neutral and refused to participate

questions.question: Why did the veteran settlement problem worsen after the Social War?
options: Most veterans had died in the fighting
The Senate refused to fund any land purchases
Veterans preferred cash payments to land grants
All public land had been sold to pay war debts
Italian land now belonged to new Roman citizens

questions.question: What imagery appeared on some coins minted by the rebel confederation?
options: A Roman eagle being struck by lightning
The goddess Roma in chains
An Italian bull goring the Roman wolf
Broken fasces symbolizing rejected authority
Two hands clasped in alliance

questions.question: How did the Social War contribute to the coming civil wars?
options: It bankrupted the Roman treasury and forced massive emergency taxation
It proved that Italian soldiers would follow their generals loyally
It permanently divided Italy into regions that remained hostile to Rome
It eliminated the Senate's ability to function as a governing institution
It introduced foreign mercenaries who later destabilized the legions
